topic.title: XSLT - The Programming Language in the XML Domain

body.p:
XSLT is very prominent in the XML domain. Outside of it, however, it is hardly known. In the TIOBE Index TIOBE Index of 2003, XSLT once ranked at position 60, the last place on the list TIOBE evidence.
UWE test footnote (2/40, callout B): DITA fn maps to UWE footnote/key/desc.

In the XML domain, however, not much would be possible without XSLT. There are some exotic application areas in which XML is efficiently processed with LISP dialects, e.g. the processing of - converted to XML - EDI X12 ASC X12 messages.
SGML, the predecessor of XML, has held its ground as the S1000D standard S1000D in the aerospace sector. Here, proprietary programming languages such as Metamorphosis are still partially used.
Alternatives to XSLT can be found in the corresponding Wikipedia article XSLT.
In this book, we focus on XSLT and XSL stylesheets - which generally refers to the combination of XSLT, XSL-FO and XPath, used to convert XML data into other formats, e.g. PDF.
For example, the car manuals of leading manufacturers are typeset using XSL, their input data prepared and transformed for further processing.

li.p: XSLT just barely qualifies as a "programming language" because you can program a Turing machine Turing machine (XSLT) with it.
This would not work with HTML or a templating language (e.g. JSP).

XSLT is not an imperative language, i.e. instructions are not executed sequentially, but rather a declarative language, i.e. for each event - or more precisely - for each DOM node traversed, a matching rule declared by the programmer is applied.
Additionally, there are functional aspects, e.g. to be able to apply the declared rules recursively. This way, larger programs can also be structured sensibly.
XSLT is often equated with XSL. But XSL XSL is more:
On one hand, there is also XPath: XPath allows complex calculations and selections on the DOM DOM nodes of an XML document.

On the other hand, XSL-FO is also part of the XSL specification XSL-FO specification. XSL-FO tags are instructions for an XSL-FO processor that generates a PDF document from an XSL-FO document. Other output formats, such as RTF, are also possible.
